name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the floor.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with an overhand grip.
Unrack the bar and hold it directly above your chest with arms fully extended.
Lower the bar slowly to your mid-chest, keeping elbows at about 45 degrees.
Press the bar back up to full extension, exhaling as you push.
formCues: Keep your feet flat on the floor throughout
Maintain a slight arch in your lower back
Squeeze your shoulder blades together before unracking
Keep your wrists straight and directly above your elbows
commonMistakes: Bouncing the bar off your chest
Lifting your hips off the bench

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench holding a dumbbell on each knee.
Lie back, bringing the dumbbells to chest height with palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells up until your arms are fully extended.
Lower them slowly back to the starting position.
formCues: Keep your elbows at 45-75 degrees from your torso
Allow a full stretch at the bottom for greater range of motion
Press the dumbbells slightly inward at the top
commonMistakes: Letting dumbbells drift too far apart
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
Going too heavy before mastering the movement

name: Incline Barbell Press
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to 30-45 degrees incline.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lower the bar to your upper chest near the clavicle.
Press back up to full extension.
formCues: Keep the angle between 30-45 degrees to target upper chest
Pull the bar down rather than just letting it fall
Keep shoulder blades pinched throughout
commonMistakes: Setting the bench too steep (turns it into a shoulder press)
Letting the bar drift too far down the chest

name: Dumbbell Flye
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding dumbbells above your chest, palms facing each other.
With a slight bend in your elbows, lower the dumbbells in a wide arc.
Lower until you feel a stretch across your chest.
Bring the dumbbells back up in the same arc, squeezing your chest at the top.
formCues: Maintain a fixed, slight elbow bend throughout
Think of hugging a large tree
Control the descent — 3 seconds down
commonMistakes: Bending the elbows too much (turns into a press)
Going too heavy and losing control
Not feeling the stretch at the bottom

name: Cable Crossover
instructions: Set cables to the highest position on both sides of a cable station.
Stand in the center, grab both handles, and step forward slightly.
With a slight elbow bend, bring your hands down and together in front of your hips.
Slowly return to the starting position with arms extended.
formCues: Keep a slight forward lean throughout
Squeeze the chest hard at the point of crossover
Control the return — don't let the weight pull you back
commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing the weight

name: Push-Up
instructions: Start in a high plank with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest to the floor by bending your elbows.
Push back up to full arm extension.
formCues: Don't let your hips sag or pike up
Keep your core braced throughout
Look slightly ahead, not straight down
commonMistakes: Not going through full range of motion

name: Decline Bench Press
instructions: Set the bench to a 15-30 degree decline and secure your feet.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lower the bar to your lower chest.
Press back up to full extension.
formCues: Keep the decline mild — 15-30 degrees is enough
Maintain shoulder blade retraction throughout
The bar should touch your lower chest/sternum area
commonMistakes: Losing shoulder blade retraction at the bottom
Bouncing the bar off the chest

name: Machine Chest Press
instructions: Adjust the seat so the handles are at chest height.
Grip the handles with palms facing forward.
Press the handles forward until arms are nearly fully extended.
Slowly return to the starting position.
formCues: Keep your back flat against the pad
Don't fully lock out elbows to maintain tension
Control the eccentric (return) portion
commonMistakes: Seat height too high or low
Moving too fast on the return

name: Chest Dip
instructions: Grip parallel bars and support your body at arm's length.
Lean forward at about 30 degrees to shift focus to chest.
Lower yourself by bending your elbows until you feel a chest stretch.
Push back up to the starting position.
formCues: Lean forward to emphasize chest over triceps
Keep elbows slightly flared, not tucked tight
Full range of motion for maximum chest activation

name: Pec Deck Machine
instructions: Sit in the machine with your back flat against the pad.
Place your forearms on the pads with elbows at 90 degrees.
Bring the pads together in front of your chest, squeezing the pecs.
Slowly return to the starting position for a full stretch.
formCues: Keep your chest up and shoulders down
Pause and squeeze at full contraction
Let the pads go back far enough to feel a stretch
commonMistakes: Not getting a full range of motion
Going too heavy and losing form

name: Deadlift
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, bar over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips and grip the bar just outside your legs.
Brace your core, chest up, and drive the floor away with your feet.
Keep the bar close to your body as you stand up fully.
Hinge back down to lower the bar under control.
formCues: Push the floor away — don't think of pulling the bar up
Keep bar in contact with your shins throughout the lift
Lock out hips and knees simultaneously at the top
Take a big breath and brace hard before each rep
commonMistakes: Bar drifting away from the body
Jerking the weight off the floor

name: Barbell Row
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, hinge forward about 45 degrees.
Grip the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Pull the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
Lower the bar under control.
formCues: Keep your back flat and parallel to the floor
Drive your elbows back, not your hands up
Initiate the pull by retracting your shoulder blades
commonMistakes: Using momentum and heaving the weight up

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Hang from a bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Engage your core and depress your shoulder blades.
Pull yourself up until your chin clears the bar.
Lower yourself slowly to a full hang.
formCues: Think about pulling your elbows toward your hips
Avoid kipping or swinging
Full dead hang at the bottom for complete range of motion
commonMistakes: Not going to full extension at the bottom

name: Lat Pulldown
instructions: Sit at the pulldown station and secure your thighs under the pads.
Grip the bar wider than shoulder-width with an overhand grip.
Lean back slightly and pull the bar down to your upper chest.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom, then slowly return the bar up.
formCues: Pull your elbows down and back, not just the bar down
Slight lean back is fine — don't go past 30 degrees
Think about putting your shoulder blades in your back pockets
commonMistakes: Pulling the bar behind the neck
Letting the weight stack crash on each rep

name: Seated Cable Row
instructions: Sit at the cable row station with feet on the platform, knees slightly bent.
Grip the handle and sit tall with your back straight.
Pull the handle toward your lower abdomen, keeping elbows close to your body.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at full contraction.
Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
formCues: Maintain an upright torso — don't lean back excessively
Drive elbows behind your body, not flared out
Pause and hold the contraction for 1 second
commonMistakes: Rocking back and forth with the torso
Letting shoulders roll forward on the return
Pulling to the chest instead of abdomen

name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
instructions: Place one knee and hand on a bench for support.
Hold a dumbbell in the opposite hand, arm hanging straight down.
Pull the dumbbell up toward your hip, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
formCues: Keep your back flat and parallel to the floor
Drive your elbow toward the ceiling, not your hand toward your chest
Rotate your torso slightly at the top for full lat contraction
commonMistakes: Rotating the torso too much (using body momentum)
Pulling the dumbbell toward the chest instead of the hip

name: T-Bar Row
instructions: Stand over the T-bar with feet straddling the bar.
Hinge forward to about 45 degrees and grip the handles.
Pull the bar toward your chest, keeping elbows close.
Lower the bar under control.
formCues: Keep your chest up and back flat
Don't jerk the weight — smooth, controlled pull
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top
commonMistakes: Not achieving full range of motion

name: Face Pull
instructions: Set a cable to upper chest height with a rope attachment.
Grip the rope with both hands and step back.
Pull the rope toward your face, flaring elbows out to the sides.
Finish with hands beside your ears and elbows pointing out.
Slowly return to the starting position.
formCues: Pull toward your nose or forehead, not your chin
Elbows should be higher than your hands at the finish
Keep your chest up and don't lean back excessively
commonMistakes: Pulling toward the chin (becomes a rear delt row)
Allowing the elbows to drop below shoulder height

name: Back Extension
instructions: Position yourself on the hyperextension bench with hips at the top pad.
Cross your arms over your chest or place hands behind your head.
Lower your torso until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings.
Raise your torso back up until your body is in a straight line.
formCues: Do not hyperextend beyond a neutral spine at the top
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement
Control the descent — don't just fall forward
commonMistakes: Hyperextending the lumbar spine at the top
Rounding the back excessively at the bottom

name: Good Morning
instructions: Place a barbell across your upper traps, feet shoulder-width apart.
With a slight knee bend, hinge forward at the hips.
Lower your torso until nearly parallel to the floor.
Drive your hips forward to return to standing.
formCues: Keep your back flat throughout — this is not a back-rounding exercise
Push your hips backward as you hinge, not your chest forward
Keep the bar in contact with your traps throughout
commonMistakes: Bending the knees too much (turns into a squat)
Using too much weight for this movement

name: Chest-Supported Row
instructions: Set an incline bench to 30-45 degrees and lie chest-down on it.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand hanging below the bench.
Row the dumbbells up toward your hips, squeezing your shoulder blades.
Lower under control.
formCues: Let your chest rest on the pad to eliminate cheating
Drive elbows back and up, not just up
Full stretch at the bottom for each rep
commonMistakes: Lifting your chest off the pad
Shrugging the shoulders up instead of retracting them
Not getting a full range of motion

name: Cable Pullover
instructions: Set a cable to the highest position with a rope or bar attachment.
Stand facing the machine and grip the attachment with both hands.
With arms mostly straight, pull the attachment down toward your thighs.
Return under control to the starting position.
formCues: Keep a slight bend in the elbows throughout
Feel the stretch in your lats at the top
Squeeze the lats hard at the bottom position
commonMistakes: Using too much weight and losing form

name: Barbell Shrug
instructions: Stand holding a barbell in front of you with an overhand grip.
With arms straight, shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears.
Hold at the top for 1-2 seconds.
Lower your shoulders back down under control.
formCues: Lift straight up — don't roll the shoulders
Hold the contraction at the top
Keep your arms straight throughout
commonMistakes: Using too much weight with poor range of motion

name: Inverted Row
instructions: Set a bar at about waist height in a squat rack.
Lie under the bar and grip it with an overhand grip.
Hang below the bar with your body in a straight line.
Pull your chest up to the bar, squeezing shoulder blades together.
Lower back down under control.
formCues: Keep your body rigid like a plank
Pull to the chest, not the neck
Make it harder by lowering the bar or elevating your feet
commonMistakes: Not achieving a full range of motion

setsRepsGuidance.bulking: 4x10 (add weight vest or lower bar for difficulty)

name: Overhead Press
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the bar at shoulder height.
Brace your core and press the bar straight up overhead.
Push your head through as the bar passes your face.
Lock out overhead with arms fully extended.
Lower the bar back to shoulder height under control.
formCues: Keep your core tight and glutes squeezed to prevent lower back arch
The bar should travel in a straight vertical line
Push your head through the 'window' at the top
commonMistakes: Pressing in front of the face instead of behind it

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing your body.
With a slight elbow bend, raise your arms out to the sides.
Lift until your arms are parallel to the floor.
Lower slowly back to the starting position.
formCues: Lead with your pinkies (internally rotate slightly) to target lateral head
Control the lowering — 3 seconds down
Don't shrug your traps up as you raise
commonMistakes: Swinging the weight up with momentum
Going too heavy and losing control
Raising arms above shoulder height unnecessarily

name: Arnold Press
instructions: Sit on a bench with dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing you.
As you press up, rotate your palms outward (facing away at the top).
Fully extend your arms overhead.
Reverse the rotation as you lower back to the starting position.
formCues: The rotation should be smooth and continuous throughout the press
Keep your core braced and don't arch your back
Control the eccentric — take 3 seconds to lower
commonMistakes: Using too much weight which limits the rotation

name: Dumbbell Front Raise
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells in front of your thighs, palms facing your body.
With a slight elbow bend, raise one or both dumbbells in front of you.
Lift to shoulder height (or slightly above).
Lower slowly back to the starting position.
formCues: Don't lean back as you raise the weight
Control the movement — no swinging
Palms can face down or toward each other

name: Reverse Dumbbell Flye
instructions: Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor.
Hold dumbbells below your chest with palms facing each other.
With a slight elbow bend, raise the dumbbells out to the sides.
Squeeze your rear delts and rhomboids at the top.
Lower under control.
formCues: Lead with your elbows, not your hands
Keep your back flat throughout
The movement is similar to spreading wings
commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing the weight up

name: Upright Row
instructions: Stand holding a barbell with an overhand grip, hands about 8-12 inches apart.
Pull the bar straight up toward your chin, leading with your elbows.
Raise until the bar is at upper chest/chin height.
Lower the bar back down under control.
formCues: Keep the bar close to your body throughout
Elbows should always be above your hands
Don't use momentum — slow and controlled
commonMistakes: Using too narrow a grip (increases impingement risk)
Pulling the bar too high above the chin
Letting the bar drift away from the body

name: Cable Lateral Raise
instructions: Set a cable to the lowest position and attach a single handle.
Stand sideways to the machine and grab the handle with your far hand.
Raise your arm out to the side to shoulder height.
Slowly lower back down.
formCues: Constant tension from the cable throughout the movement
Slight forward lean to better target the lateral head
Don't shrug your shoulder at the top
commonMistakes: Standing too close to the machine

name: Machine Shoulder Press
instructions: Adjust the seat so handles are at shoulder level.
Grip the handles and press upward until arms are extended.
Lower back to shoulder level under control.
formCues: Keep your back flat against the pad
Don't fully lock out your elbows
Exhale as you press up
commonMistakes: Arching away from the back pad
Using too much weight and losing posture
Going too fast on the eccentric

name: Barbell Curl
instructions: Stand holding a barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keeping your elbows pinned at your sides, curl the bar up to shoulder height.
Squeeze your biceps at the top.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
formCues: Keep your upper arms completely still
Don't swing your hips or lean back
Take 2-3 seconds to lower the weight
commonMistakes: Swinging the body to lift the weight
Not achieving full range of motion

name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides, palms facing forward.
Keeping your elbows stationary, curl both dumbbells up toward your shoulders.
Squeeze your biceps at the top.
Lower slowly back to full extension.
formCues: Supinate (rotate) your wrists as you curl for peak contraction
Keep your elbows stationary at your sides
Full extension at the bottom for complete stretch

name: Hammer Curl
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides, palms facing each other (neutral grip).
Curl the dumbbells upward without rotating your wrists.
Squeeze at the top.
Lower back down slowly.
formCues: Keep palms facing each other throughout the movement
Elbows stay fixed at your sides
This hits the brachialis and brachioradialis more than a regular curl
commonMistakes: Rotating the wrist (turning it into a regular curl)
Using momentum to swing the weight

name: Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Set a cable to the highest position with a straight bar or rope attachment.
Grip the bar with an overhand grip, elbows tucked at your sides.
Push the bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly return to the starting position.
formCues: Keep your elbows stationary and pinned to your sides
Fully extend the arms at the bottom for peak contraction
Don't lean forward excessively
commonMistakes: Letting elbows flare out or drift forward
Using body weight to push down

name: Skull Crusher
instructions: Lie on a bench holding an EZ-bar or straight bar with arms extended above your chest.
Keeping your upper arms perpendicular to the floor, bend your elbows.
Lower the bar toward your forehead.
Extend your arms back to the starting position.
formCues: Upper arms remain fixed — only the forearms move
Keep your elbows shoulder-width apart, don't let them flare
Control the descent — this is a vulnerable position
commonMistakes: Moving the upper arms instead of just the forearms

name: Tricep Dip
instructions: Place your hands on a bench behind you, feet on the floor.
Lower your hips toward the floor by bending your elbows.
Keep your back close to the bench.
Push back up to the starting position.
formCues: Keep your hips close to the bench to target triceps
Don't shrug your shoulders up
Keep elbows pointing straight back, not out
commonMistakes: Hips drifting too far from the bench

name: Overhead Tricep Extension
instructions: Hold a dumbbell with both hands and raise it overhead.
Keeping your upper arms beside your head, bend your elbows.
Lower the dumbbell behind your head until you feel a stretch.
Extend your arms back to the starting position.
formCues: Keep your elbows pointing toward the ceiling
Don't let the elbows flare out to the sides
Get a full stretch at the bottom for the long head
commonMistakes: Moving the upper arms during the exercise

name: Preacher Curl
instructions: Sit at the preacher bench and place your upper arms on the pad.
Grip the bar with an underhand grip.
Curl the bar upward, stopping just before vertical.
Lower slowly under control.
formCues: Keep your upper arms pressed firmly against the pad
Don't fully lock out at the bottom — keep tension on the biceps
Control the eccentric portion
commonMistakes: Letting arms slip off the pad
Locking out at the bottom (hyperextension risk)

name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions: Place the barbell on your upper traps and stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Brace your core and take a deep breath.
Push your knees out and descend by sitting back and down.
Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to return to standing.
formCues: Knees should track over your toes throughout
Keep your chest up and torso as upright as possible
Push the floor away — don't think of standing up

name: Front Squat
instructions: Rest the barbell on your front deltoids, elbows high and forward.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Descend by sitting straight down, keeping a very upright torso.
Lower until thighs are parallel or below.
Drive through your heels to stand.
formCues: Keep elbows up — if they drop, the bar will fall
Much more upright torso than a back squat
Knees should travel forward over toes more than in back squat
commonMistakes: Elbows dropping and bar rolling forward

name: Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold a kettlebell vertically at your chest with both hands.
Stand with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, toes turned out.
Sit down between your heels, keeping your chest up.
Drive through your heels to return to standing.
formCues: Use your elbows to push your knees out at the bottom
Keep the kettlebell close to your chest throughout
A great teaching tool for squat mechanics

name: Leg Press
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine with your back flat against the pad.
Place your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform.
Release the safety handles and lower the platform toward your chest.
Press the platform back up until your legs are nearly fully extended.
Re-engage safety handles before exiting.
formCues: Don't lock your knees out at the top
Keep your lower back pressed against the pad throughout
Foot position changes emphasis: high feet = more glutes/hamstrings, low feet = more quads
commonMistakes: Going too heavy and having knees cave
Letting lower back round off the pad

name: Barbell Lunge
instructions: Place the barbell across your upper traps.
Step forward with one leg and lower your back knee toward the floor.
Keep your front knee tracking over your toes.
Push through your front heel to return to standing.
Repeat on the other side.
formCues: Keep your torso upright throughout
Front knee should not go past your toes
Back knee hovers just above the floor

name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand holding a barbell with an overhand grip, feet hip-width apart.
With a slight knee bend, hinge forward by pushing your hips back.
Lower the bar along your legs until you feel a deep hamstring stretch.
Drive your hips forward to return to standing.
formCues: Push your hips back — don't just bend over
Keep the bar close to your legs throughout
Maintain a neutral spine — no rounding
commonMistakes: Bending the knees too much (turning it into a deadlift)

name: Lying Leg Curl
instructions: Lie face down on the leg curl machine with the pad just below your calves.
Curl your heels toward your glutes.
Pause at the top, squeezing your hamstrings.
Lower slowly back to the starting position.
formCues: Hips stay flat on the pad throughout
Control the eccentric — 3 seconds down
Point toes away to increase hamstring activation
commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing the weight
Not achieving full range of motion

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench.
Roll a barbell over your hips.
Drive your hips upward by squeezing your glutes.
Your upper back should pivot on the bench.
Lower your hips back toward the floor.
formCues: Chin to chest at the top to prevent back extension
Drive through your heels, not your toes
Fully extend hips at the top — get a hard glute squeeze
commonMistakes: Pushing through the toes instead of heels
Not fully extending at the top

name: Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Drive your hips up toward the ceiling by squeezing your glutes.
Hold at the top for 1-2 seconds.
Lower your hips back to the floor.
formCues: Squeeze glutes hard at the top
Feet should be hip-width apart
Don't let your back arch excessively
commonMistakes: Pushing through the toes instead of heels
Hamstrings doing the work instead of glutes
Not fully extending at the top

name: Standing Calf Raise
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or calf raise machine, heels hanging off.
Rise up onto your toes as high as possible.
Pause at the top.
Lower your heels below the platform level for a full stretch.
formCues: Full range of motion — get the heel all the way down
Pause at the top and squeeze the calf hard
Straight legs target the gastrocnemius more
commonMistakes: Not going through full range of motion

name: Seated Calf Raise
instructions: Sit in the seated calf raise machine with the pad on your lower thighs.
Rise up onto your toes as high as possible.
Pause and squeeze at the top.
Lower your heels to get a full stretch.
formCues: Bent knees target the soleus (deeper calf muscle)
Full range of motion is critical
Slow, controlled reps are more effective than bouncing
commonMistakes: Not feeling a stretch at the bottom

name: Leg Extension
instructions: Sit in the leg extension machine with the pad on your shins.
Extend your legs until nearly fully straight.
Pause and squeeze your quads at the top.
Lower slowly back to the starting position.
formCues: Pause at the top for peak quad contraction
Control the eccentric — don't just let the weight fall
Keep your back against the pad
commonMistakes: Swinging the legs up with momentum
Not fully extending at the top

name: Sumo Deadlift
instructions: Stand with feet much wider than shoulder-width, toes pointed out 45 degrees.
Grip the bar inside your legs with a double overhand or mixed grip.
Keep your torso more upright than a conventional deadlift.
Drive the floor away and stand up fully.
Lower the bar under control.
formCues: Push your knees out over your toes throughout
More upright torso compared to conventional
Spread the floor apart with your feet as you lift
commonMistakes: Hips shooting up before the bar leaves the floor

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Stand with your back to a bench, holding dumbbells at your sides.
Place one foot on the bench behind you.
Lower your back knee toward the floor.
Drive through your front heel to stand back up.
formCues: Keep your front foot far enough forward that your knee stays over your toes
Keep your torso upright
Control the descent — don't just drop down
commonMistakes: Foot too close to the bench causing knee to go past toes

name: Dumbbell Step-Up
instructions: Hold dumbbells at your sides and stand in front of a bench or box.
Step up with one foot, pressing through the heel to stand fully.
Bring your other foot up to the bench.
Step back down with the same leg.
formCues: Drive through the heel of the leg on the bench
Don't push off with the back foot
Keep your torso upright
commonMistakes: Pushing off the back foot (reduces stimulus on the working leg)
Using a platform that's too low

name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
instructions: Kneel on a mat with your ankles anchored under a bar or held by a partner.
Keep your body straight from knees to head.
Slowly lower your torso toward the floor by allowing your knees to extend.
Catch yourself with your hands if needed, then curl back up using your hamstrings.
formCues: The eccentric (lowering) is where most of the benefit comes from
Keep your hips extended — don't bend at the hips
This is extremely difficult — most people need to start with slow eccentrics only
commonMistakes: Going too fast and not controlling the descent

name: Wall Sit
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall.
Slide down until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Hold this position for the target duration.
Push through your heels and stand back up.
formCues: Thighs should be parallel to the floor
Keep your back flat against the wall
Don't rest your hands on your knees

name: Cable Glute Kickback
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable.
Face the machine holding the frame for balance.
Kick your working leg straight back and up, squeezing your glute.
Return to the starting position under control.
formCues: Keep a slight bend in the standing knee
Don't rotate your hip — kick straight back
Squeeze the glute hard at the top of the movement
commonMistakes: Using too much weight and losing control

name: Adductor Machine
instructions: Sit in the adductor machine with the pads on the insides of your knees.
Start with legs spread wide.
Squeeze your legs together against the resistance.
Slowly return to the starting position.
formCues: Keep your back flat against the pad
Controlled movement — no jerking
Full range of motion in both directions
commonMistakes: Using momentum to bring the legs together
Not getting a full stretch at the start

name: Plank
instructions: Start in a push-up position and lower onto your forearms.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core and glutes.
Hold for the target duration.
formCues: Don't let your hips sag or pike up
Squeeze your glutes and brace your abs like you're about to be punched
Look at the floor to keep your neck neutral

name: Crunch
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands behind your head without pulling your neck.
Curl your shoulders off the floor by contracting your abs.
Hold the contraction briefly, then lower back down.
formCues: Focus on curling your ribcage toward your pelvis
Don't pull on your neck with your hands
Exhale as you crunch up
commonMistakes: Pulling the neck with the hands
Using hip flexors instead of abs

name: Russian Twist
instructions: Sit on the floor with knees bent, lean back to about 45 degrees.
Hold your hands together or hold a weight in front of you.
Rotate your torso to one side, then the other.
Each side rotation counts as one rep.
formCues: The rotation comes from your torso, not your arms
Keep your feet elevated for more difficulty
Keep your lower back from rounding
commonMistakes: Moving the arms instead of rotating the torso
Going through the motion without core engagement

name: Hanging Leg Raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip.
Engage your core and raise your legs straight up to 90 degrees.
Hold briefly at the top.
Lower your legs slowly without swinging.
formCues: No swinging — control the movement completely
Posterior pelvic tilt at the top for more ab activation
Beginners can bend the knees to make it easier
commonMistakes: Using hip flexors exclusively without involving the abs

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
instructions: Kneel on a mat holding the ab wheel in front of you.
Roll the wheel forward, extending your body toward the floor.
Extend as far as you can while maintaining a neutral spine.
Pull the wheel back toward your knees using your abs and lats.
formCues: Don't let your lower back arch — keep it neutral
Pull the wheel back using your abs, not your hip flexors
Start with short range of motion and increase gradually
commonMistakes: Using arm strength instead of core

name: Bicycle Crunch
instructions: Lie on your back and bring your knees to your chest.
Place your hands behind your head.
Extend one leg out while rotating your upper body toward the opposite knee.
Alternate sides in a pedaling motion.
formCues: The rotation should come from your torso, not your elbows
Keep the movement slow and controlled for maximum activation
Keep the lower back pressed to the floor
commonMistakes: Moving elbows instead of rotating the torso
Going too fast and losing the mind-muscle connection

name: Cable Crunch
instructions: Kneel in front of a high cable with a rope attachment.
Hold the rope at the sides of your head.
Crunch downward by flexing your spine, bringing your elbows toward your knees.
Slowly return to the starting position.
formCues: Hinge at the hips AND flex the spine — it's not just a hip hinge
Keep your hips stationary throughout
Really focus on curling the ribcage toward the pelvis
commonMistakes: Hinging at the hips only (turning it into a lat pulldown)
Moving the hips forward and back
Not getting a full range of motion

name: Dead Bug
instructions: Lie on your back, arms extended toward the ceiling, knees bent 90 degrees.
Press your lower back into the floor and maintain that pressure.
Slowly extend one arm back and the opposite leg out simultaneously.
Return and repeat on the other side.
formCues: Lower back must stay pressed to the floor throughout
Move slowly and deliberately
Breathe out as you extend the limbs
commonMistakes: Lower back arching off the floor
Not fully extending the arm and leg

name: Side Plank
instructions: Lie on your side and prop yourself up on your forearm.
Stack your feet or stagger them for stability.
Raise your hips off the floor to form a straight line from head to heels.
Hold for the target duration.
formCues: Keep your hips from rotating forward or backward
Drive your hip up toward the ceiling — don't let it sag
Keep your neck neutral

name: Burpee
instructions: Start standing, then squat down and place hands on the floor.
Jump your feet back into a push-up position.
Perform a push-up.
Jump your feet back toward your hands.
Explode upward into a jump with arms overhead.
formCues: Keep your core engaged throughout
Land softly from the jump to protect your joints
Set a sustainable pace rather than sprinting and stopping
commonMistakes: Skipping the push-up for a scaled version without acknowledging it
Collapsing the hips during the push-up phase

name: Clean and Press
instructions: Stand with the bar at mid-shin, grip just outside legs.
Explosively pull the bar upward, using hip extension and a high pull.
Drop under the bar and catch it at shoulder height (front rack position).
From the front rack, press the bar overhead to full extension.
Lower back to the starting position.
formCues: The power comes from the hips, not the arms
Stay on your heels through the pull phase
A strong front rack position is essential for a safe press
commonMistakes: Pulling with the arms too early
A poor front rack position (bar resting on hands instead of shoulders)

name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, kettlebell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and grip the kettlebell.
Hike the kettlebell back between your legs.
Drive your hips forward explosively to swing the kettlebell to chest height.
Let the kettlebell swing back and repeat.
formCues: This is a hip hinge, not a squat — don't bend the knees excessively
Power comes entirely from the hip snap
Your arms are just a guide — don't use them to lift the weight
commonMistakes: Using arms to lift the kettlebell

name: Thruster
instructions: Hold the barbell in a front rack position at shoulder height.
Squat down until thighs are parallel to the floor.
Use the momentum from standing up to drive the bar overhead.
Lock out arms overhead at the top.
Lower bar back to shoulders and repeat.
formCues: The transition from squat to press should be seamless
Drive from the hips to add momentum to the press
Strong front rack and core brace are essential
commonMistakes: Pausing at the top of the squat before pressing
Pressing independently without using leg drive

name: Farmer's Carry
instructions: Pick up a heavy dumbbell in each hand.
Stand tall with your shoulders back and core braced.
Walk forward for the target distance or time.
Keep your steps controlled and your posture tall throughout.
formCues: Don't let the dumbbells cause you to lean to one side
Keep your shoulders packed down and back
Short, controlled steps
commonMistakes: Taking too large steps and losing stability

name: Battle Ropes
instructions: Anchor a thick rope to a fixed point at the base.
Hold one end in each hand, stand with a slight squat.
Create alternating waves by raising and lowering your arms.
Maintain the waves for the target duration.
formCues: Stay low in your squat throughout for lower body engagement
The power comes from the whole body, not just the arms
Keep your core braced and back straight
commonMistakes: Only using arms without body drive

name: Box Jump
instructions: Stand facing a sturdy box or platform.
Bend your knees and swing your arms back.
Explode upward, swinging arms forward for momentum.
Land softly on the box with both feet, absorbing the impact through your legs.
Step down carefully.
formCues: Land softly — bend knees to absorb the impact
Full hip extension at the top of the jump
Step down, don't jump down, to reduce joint stress
commonMistakes: Using a box that is too high before mastering the pattern

name: Dumbbell Pull and Press
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells, hinge forward and row them to your hips.
Stand up and curl the dumbbells to your shoulders.
Press overhead to full extension.
Reverse the movement to the starting position.
formCues: Each phase should be deliberate and controlled
Full extension on the press
Keep your core engaged throughout
commonMistakes: Arching the back during the press

name: Man Maker
instructions: Start standing, holding dumbbells.
Squat down and place dumbbells on floor.
Jump feet back to push-up position.
Perform a push-up.
Row each dumbbell while in plank.
Jump feet forward and clean dumbbells to shoulders.
Press overhead.
formCues: Control each phase — this is a skill movement
Core stays braced throughout especially during the row
Maintain neutral spine in the plank position
commonMistakes: Hips rising too high in the plank position

name: Resistance Band Squat
instructions: Place a resistance band just above your knees.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Push your knees out against the band resistance as you squat down.
Drive back up to standing, maintaining outward knee pressure.
formCues: Actively push knees outward against the band
This improves glute activation and knee tracking
Keep your chest up and heels flat
commonMistakes: Letting the band collapse the knees inward
Not maintaining tension against the band

name: Resistance Band Pull-Apart
instructions: Hold a resistance band with both hands at shoulder height, arms extended.
Pull the band apart by moving your hands out to the sides.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at full extension.
Return slowly to the starting position.
formCues: Keep your arms straight throughout
Squeeze the rear delts hard at full extension
Control the return — don't let the band snap back
commonMistakes: Not achieving full range of motion

name: Resistance Band Bicep Curl
instructions: Stand on the middle of a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold an end in each hand at your sides.
Curl your hands toward your shoulders against the band resistance.
Lower slowly back down.
formCues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides
The resistance increases as you curl — maintain control
Full extension at the bottom
commonMistakes: Using a band with too little resistance

name: Kettlebell Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold a kettlebell by the horns at chest height.
Stand with feet slightly wider than hip-width, toes turned out.
Sit down between your heels into a deep squat.
Drive through your heels to stand.
formCues: Use elbows to push knees outward at the bottom
Chest stays tall throughout
Try to achieve full depth

name: Turkish Get-Up
instructions: Lie on the floor with a kettlebell in one hand, pressed toward the ceiling.
Using a series of controlled movements, rise from lying to standing.
Keep the kettlebell locked out overhead throughout.
Reverse the sequence back to the floor.
formCues: Eyes on the kettlebell at all times
Move slowly and deliberately — this is a skill movement
Each position should feel stable before moving to the next

name: Mountain Climber
instructions: Start in a high push-up position with hands under shoulders.
Drive one knee toward your chest.
Quickly switch legs in a running motion.
Continue alternating at a controlled or fast pace.
formCues: Keep your hips level — don't let them rise
Brace your core throughout
Shoulders stay over your hands
commonMistakes: Not fully driving the knee to the chest

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells in front of your thighs.
Hinge at the hips, pushing them back.
Lower dumbbells along the fronts of your legs until you feel a hamstring stretch.
Drive hips forward to return to standing.
formCues: Soft knee bend throughout
Keep dumbbells close to your legs
Flat back — no rounding

name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Set a bench to a 45-60 degree incline and sit back on it.
Let your arms hang straight down, dumbbells at your sides.
Curl both dumbbells up, supinating your wrists.
Lower slowly back to the starting position.
formCues: The incline increases the range of motion and stretch on the biceps
Let the arms fully extend at the bottom for maximum stretch
Don't swing the torso
commonMistakes: Swinging the torso to assist the lift

name: Single-Arm Cable Row
instructions: Set a cable to lower chest height with a single handle attachment.
Sit or stand sideways and grip the handle.
Row the handle toward your hip, rotating slightly for a full contraction.
Return under control.
formCues: Allow a slight torso rotation to maximize the range of motion
Drive the elbow behind you, not just your hand toward your hip
Initiate by retracting the shoulder blade
commonMistakes: Not allowing any rotation (limits range of motion)
Rotating too much and turning it into a cheat row
Using arm strength instead of pulling with the back

name: Concentration Curl
instructions: Sit on a bench, lean forward, and brace your elbow on the inside of your thigh.
Hold a dumbbell in that hand.
Curl the dumbbell up toward your shoulder.
Lower slowly back down.
formCues: The thigh is just a support — don't actively push against it
Full supination at the top for peak contraction
Slow, deliberate reps
commonMistakes: Swinging the arm away from the thigh
Not supinating the wrist at the top

name: Cable Bicep Curl
instructions: Set a cable to the lowest position with a straight bar or EZ attachment.
Stand facing the machine and grip the attachment.
Curl upward to shoulder height.
Lower slowly under constant tension.
formCues: Constant cable tension differs from free weights — embrace the full ROM
Keep elbows stationary at your sides
The eccentric under constant tension is very effective
commonMistakes: Elbows drifting forward at the top
Not taking advantage of the constant tension

name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
instructions: Set a cable to the highest position with a straight bar.
Stand with a slight forward lean and grip the bar.
With arms straight, pull the bar down to your thighs.
Return slowly to the starting position.
formCues: Arms stay straight throughout — this is a lat isolation exercise
Hinge slightly forward at the hips for a better angle
Feel the lats stretch at the top
commonMistakes: Not getting a full stretch at the top

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support, hold dumbbells at shoulder height.
Press both dumbbells upward until arms are fully extended.
Lower back to shoulder height under control.
formCues: Keep your back pressed against the bench
Don't fully lock out your elbows at the top
Exhale on the way up
commonMistakes: Arching the lower back off the bench
Using too much weight and losing control
Pressing at an angle rather than straight up

name: Resistance Band Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit with your upper back against a bench, band across your hips anchored under feet.
Drive your hips upward against the band resistance.
Squeeze your glutes at the top.
Lower hips back down.
formCues: The band increases resistance at the top where glutes are strongest
Chin to chest to prevent lower back extension
Full hip extension at the top

name: Push Press
instructions: Hold the bar in a front rack position at shoulder height.
Perform a slight knee dip.
Drive through your legs and press the bar overhead explosively.
Lock out overhead and lower back to shoulders.
formCues: The knee dip should be quick and shallow — just enough to create drive
The leg drive transitions into arm press seamlessly
Lock out fully overhead
commonMistakes: Too deep of a dip (becomes a push jerk)
Pushing the bar forward instead of straight up
Not using leg drive and just pressing

name: Cable Upright Row
instructions: Set a cable to the lowest position with a straight bar.
Stand and grip the bar with hands about shoulder-width apart.
Pull the bar upward toward your chin, elbows leading.
Lower slowly back to the starting position.
formCues: Elbows should always be above hands
Keep the bar close to your body
Don't pull excessively high
commonMistakes: Pulling too high causing shoulder impingement

name: Decline Crunch
instructions: Sit on a decline bench and secure your feet.
Lie back and place hands behind your head.
Crunch your torso up toward your knees.
Lower slowly back down.
formCues: The decline increases the range of motion for the abs
Don't pull on the neck
Focus on spinal flexion rather than just sitting up
commonMistakes: Using hip flexors to pull up
Not getting full range of motion

name: V-Up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with arms extended overhead.
Simultaneously raise your legs and torso to form a V shape.
Try to touch your toes at the top.
Lower back down slowly.
formCues: Keep your legs straight throughout
The movement should be smooth, not jerky
Exhale as you come up
commonMistakes: Using momentum instead of core strength

name: Cable Woodchop
instructions: Set a cable to the highest position with a single handle.
Stand sideways to the machine and grip the handle with both hands.
Pull the handle diagonally downward toward the opposite hip.
Rotate your torso and pivot your back foot.
Return to the starting position.
formCues: The movement comes from the torso rotation, not the arms
Keep your arms relatively straight
Pivot your back foot to allow full rotation
commonMistakes: Using the arms to pull instead of rotating

name: Treadmill
instructions: Start the treadmill at a comfortable walking pace.
Warm up for 3-5 minutes at an easy pace.
Gradually increase speed or incline to your target effort.
Maintain an upright posture and relaxed arm swing.
Cool down for the last 3-5 minutes at a slow pace.

name: Stationary Bike
instructions: Adjust the seat so your knee is slightly bent at the bottom of the pedal stroke.
Warm up at a light resistance for 3-5 minutes.
Increase resistance or cadence to your target effort.
Keep your core engaged and back relatively flat.
Cool down at light resistance for the last few minutes.

name: Elliptical
instructions: Step onto the pedals and grasp the handles lightly.
Begin a smooth, gliding motion.
Keep an upright posture and engage your core.
Adjust resistance and incline to your target effort.
Cool down with an easy 3-5 minute glide.

name: Rowing Machine
instructions: Strap your feet in and grasp the handle with an overhand grip.
Drive with the legs first, then lean back, then pull the handle to your ribs.
Reverse the motion under control: arms, torso, legs.
Keep your back neutral and core engaged throughout.
Maintain a smooth rhythm rather than jerky pulls.

name: Stair Climber
instructions: Step on and hold the handrails lightly for balance only.
Begin at a slow pace and gradually increase speed.
Take full steps rather than quick shallow ones.
Keep your torso upright; avoid leaning on the rails.
Cool down at a slow pace before stepping off.

name: Jump Rope
instructions: Adjust rope length so the handles reach your armpits when standing on the middle of the rope.
Keep elbows close to your body and rotate the rope with your wrists.
Land softly on the balls of your feet.
Maintain a relaxed, upright posture.
Alternate work and rest if doing intervals.

name: Running
instructions: Warm up with a 3-5 minute easy jog.
Maintain an upright posture and relaxed shoulders.
Land with your foot under your hip, not out in front.
Breathe in a steady rhythm you can sustain.
Cool down with a slow jog or walk.

name: Cycling
instructions: Ensure the saddle height is adjusted so your knee is slightly bent at the bottom of the pedal stroke.
Warm up at an easy cadence for 5 minutes.
Maintain a cadence of 80-100 RPM at moderate effort.
Keep your core engaged and shoulders relaxed.
Cool down at easy cadence before finishing.
